---
title: "WGCNA"
author: "David"
date: "2026-04-04"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
library('WGCNA')
library(readr)
Dineen_rlog_counts <- read_csv("Dineen_rlog_counts.csv")
Dineen_rlog_counts_t <- Dineen_rlog_counts %>% column_to_rownames('wbid') %>% t() %>% as.data.frame()
rownames(Dineen_rlog_counts_t) <- gsub("sorted_", "", rownames(Dineen_rlog_counts_t))
Dineen_rlog_counts_t[1:10,1:10]

# remove least variant
# Calculate variance for each gene
gene_vars <- apply(Dineen_rlog_transposed, 2, var)

# Remove genes with variance below threshold .1
var_threshold <- .1
Dineen_filtered <- Dineen_rlog_counts_t[, gene_vars > var_threshold]
dim(Dineen_filtered)
# Check how many genes removed
ncol(Dineen_rlog_counts_t) - ncol(Dineen_filtered)

```

```{r WGCNA filtering}
allowWGCNAThreads()

# Check for genes and samples with too many missing values or zero variance
gsg <- goodSamplesGenes(Dineen_filtered, verbose = 3)
gsg$allOK  # Should return TRUE
```

```{r power threshold for scale-free}
# Calculate soft-thresholding powers
powers <- c(1:10, seq(12, 20, by = 2))
sft <- pickSoftThreshold(Dineen_filtered, powerVector = powers, verbose = 5)
sft
```
```{r power threshold for unsigned}
sft_unsigned <- pickSoftThreshold(Dineen_filtered,
                                    networkType = "unsigned",
                                    powerVector = powers,
                                    verbose = 5)
sft_unsigned
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r }
# Set the soft power
softPower <- 12

# Calculate adjacency matrix
adjacency <- adjacency(Dineen_filtered, power = softPower, type = "signed")

# Check dimensions
dim(adjacency)  # Should be 5967 x 5967 (gene x gene)

# Transform adjacency into Topological Overlap Matrix (TOM)
TOM <- TOMsimilarity(adjacency, TOMType = "signed")

# Calculate dissimilarity for clustering
dissTOM <- 1 - TOM

# Check progress
cat("Adjacency and TOM calculated\n")

# Cluster genes using TOM-based dissimilarity
geneTree <- hclust(as.dist(dissTOM), method = "average")

# Plot the dendrogram
plot(geneTree, xlab = "", sub = "",
     main = "Gene clustering on TOM-based dissimilarity",
     labels = FALSE, hang = 0.04)

```

```{r module detection}
# Set minimum module size
  minModuleSize <- 30  # Genes per module minimum

  # Module identification using dynamic tree cut
  dynamicMods <- cutreeDynamic(dendro = geneTree,
                               distM = dissTOM,
                               deepSplit = 2,  # Sensitivity: 0-4, higher = more/smaller modules
                               pamRespectsDendro = FALSE,
                               minClusterSize = minModuleSize)

  # Check how many modules were found
  table(dynamicMods)

  # Convert numeric labels to colors (WGCNA convention)
  dynamicColors <- labels2colors(dynamicMods)
  table(dynamicColors)

  # Plot the dendrogram with module colors
  plotDendroAndColors(geneTree, dynamicColors, "Dynamic Tree Cut",
                     dendroLabels = FALSE, hang = 0.03,
                     addGuide = TRUE, guideHang = 0.05,
                     main = "Gene dendrogram and module colors")
```
```{r Step 6: Calculate Module Eigengenes and Merge Similar Modules}
# Calculate eigengenes (1st principal component of each module)
  MEList <- moduleEigengenes(Dineen_filtered, colors = dynamicColors)
  MEs <- MEList$eigengenes

  # Calculate dissimilarity of module eigengenes
  MEDiss <- 1 - cor(MEs)

  # Cluster module eigengenes
  METree <- hclust(as.dist(MEDiss), method = "average")

  # Plot the dendrogram of modules
  plot(METree, main = "Clustering of module eigengenes",
       xlab = "", sub = "")

  # Set a height cutoff for merging (0.25 = modules with >75% correlation get merged)
  MEDissThres <- 0.25
  abline(h = MEDissThres, col = "red")
```

```{r }
# Call the merge function
  merge <- mergeCloseModules(Dineen_filtered,
                            dynamicColors,
                            cutHeight = MEDissThres,
                            verbose = 3)

  # The merged module colors
  mergedColors <- merge$colors
  table(mergedColors)

  # Eigengenes of the merged modules
  mergedMEs <- merge$newMEs

  # Compare before and after merging
  plotDendroAndColors(geneTree,
                     cbind(dynamicColors, mergedColors),
                     c("Dynamic Tree Cut", "Merged dynamic"),
                     dendroLabels = FALSE, hang = 0.03,
                     addGuide = TRUE, guideHang = 0.05,
                     main = "Gene dendrogram: before and after merging")
```


```{r predict genotypes}

  # Create trait data frame
  # Rows must match samples in Dineen_filtered exactly
  sample_names <- rownames(Dineen_filtered)

  traits <- data.frame(
    row.names = sample_names,
    # Binary traits for each genotype
    WT = as.numeric(grepl("^wt_", sample_names)),
    elt2d = as.numeric(grepl("^elt2d_", sample_names) & !grepl("elt2delt7d", sample_names)),
    elt7d = as.numeric(grepl("^elt7d_", sample_names)),
    double = as.numeric(grepl("elt2delt7d", sample_names))
  )

  # Check it looks correct
  traits
  
  nGenes <- ncol(Dineen_filtered)
  nSamples <- nrow(Dineen_filtered)

  # Recalculate MEs with merged colors (if not already done)
  MEs0 <- moduleEigengenes(Dineen_filtered, mergedColors)$eigengenes
  MEs <- orderMEs(MEs0)  # Order by hierarchical clustering

  # Correlate eigengenes with traits
  moduleTraitCor <- cor(MEs, traits, use = "p")
  moduleTraitPvalue <- corPvalueStudent(moduleTraitCor, nSamples)

  # Display correlations
  moduleTraitCor
  moduleTraitPvalue

```

```{r heatmap}
  # Heatmap of module-trait relationships
  textMatrix <- paste(signif(moduleTraitCor, 2), "\n(",
                     signif(moduleTraitPvalue, 1), ")", sep = "")
  dim(textMatrix) <- dim(moduleTraitCor)

  par(mar = c(6, 8.5, 3, 3))
  labeledHeatmap(Matrix = moduleTraitCor,
                xLabels = names(traits),
                yLabels = names(MEs),
                ySymbols = names(MEs),
                colorLabels = FALSE,
                colors = blueWhiteRed(50),
                textMatrix = textMatrix,
                setStdMargins = FALSE,
                cex.text = 0.5,
                zlim = c(-1, 1),
                main = paste("Module-trait relationships"))
```

```{r comparison with DE classes}
 # Get gene-to-module assignments
  gene_modules <- data.frame(
    wbid = colnames(Dineen_filtered),
    wgcna_module = mergedColors,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  # Load DESeq2 buffering analysis
  buffering <- read.csv("Dineen_DESeq2_buffering_analysis.csv")

  # Merge with WGCNA modules
  comparison <- buffering %>%
    select(wbid, buffering_pattern, elt2d_log2FC, double_log2FC, interaction_log2FC) %>%
    inner_join(gene_modules, by = "wbid")

  # How many genes overlap?
  nrow(comparison)  # Should be close to 5967

  # Contingency table
  overlap_table <- table(comparison$wgcna_module, comparison$buffering_pattern)
  overlap_table

  # As proportions (% of each module in each DESeq2 class)
  prop.table(overlap_table, margin = 1) * 100  # Row percentages

  

  library(ggplot2)

  # Heatmap of overlap
  overlap_df <- as.data.frame(overlap_table)
  colnames(overlap_df) <- c("WGCNA_Module", "DESeq2_Pattern", "Count")

  ggplot(overlap_df, aes(x = DESeq2_Pattern, y = WGCNA_Module, fill = Count)) +
    geom_tile() +
    geom_text(aes(label = Count), size = 3) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "darkblue") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
    labs(title = "WGCNA Module vs DESeq2 Buffering Pattern Overlap",
         x = "DESeq2 Buffering Pattern",
         y = "WGCNA Module")
```
```{r plot data}
# Prepare expression matrix (genes as rows, samples as columns)
  expr_matrix <- t(Dineen_filtered)

# Scale by row (z-score)
  expr_scaled <- t(scale(t(expr_matrix)))

  # Make sure comparison dataframe has all genes and is ordered correctly
  comparison <- comparison[match(rownames(expr_matrix), comparison$wbid), ]

  # Create row annotations for WGCNA modules
  module_colors <- comparison$wgcna_module
  names(module_colors) <- comparison$wbid

  # Create row annotations for DESeq2 patterns
  buffering_colors <- comparison$buffering_pattern

  # Define colors for buffering patterns
  buffering_palette <- c(
    "ELT7_buffers_upregulation" = "#E31A1C",
    "ELT7_buffers_downregulation" = "#1F78B4",
    "ELT7_compensatory" = "#33A02C",
    "ELT7_reveals_new_targets" = "#FF7F00",
    "ELT2_dominant_no_ELT7_effect" = "#999999",
    "other" = "#FFFFFF"
  )

  # Create row annotations
  row_ha_left <- rowAnnotation(
    WGCNA = module_colors,
    col = list(WGCNA = setNames(unique(module_colors), unique(module_colors))),
    annotation_name_side = "top"
  )

  row_ha_right <- rowAnnotation(
    DESeq2 = buffering_colors,
    col = list(DESeq2 = buffering_palette),
    annotation_name_side = "top"
  )

  # Create column annotations for genotypes
  col_ha <- HeatmapAnnotation(
    Genotype = c(rep("WT", 4), rep("elt7d", 3), rep("elt2d", 4), rep("double", 3)),
    col = list(Genotype = c("WT" = "#4DAF4A", "elt2d" = "#E41A1C",
                            "elt7d" = "#377EB8", "double" = "#984EA3"))
  )

```

```{r heatmap}
  # Create the heatmap
  # Scale by row (z-score) to see patterns better
  ht <- Heatmap(expr_scaled,
                name = "Expression\n(z-score)",

                # Clustering
                cluster_rows = TRUE,
                cluster_columns = FALSE,  # Keep samples in genotype order
                show_row_dend = TRUE,
                show_column_dend = FALSE,
                
                # Split by WGCNA module
                row_split = factor(module_colors, levels = unique(module_colors)),
                row_title_rot = 0,
                row_gap = unit(2, "mm"),

                # Annotations
                left_annotation = row_ha_left,
                right_annotation = row_ha_right,
                top_annotation = col_ha,

                # Labels
                show_row_names = FALSE,  # Too many genes to show names
                show_column_names = TRUE,
                column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),

                # Colors
                col = colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), c("blue", "white", "red")),

                # Size
                heatmap_legend_param = list(
                  title = "Expression\n(z-score)",
                  at = c(-2, 0, 2),
                  labels = c("-2", "0", "2")
                )
  )

  # Draw the heatmap
  draw(ht, heatmap_legend_side = "right")
```

```{r heatmap group by DE}
# Prepare expression matrix (genes as rows, samples as columns)
  expr_matrix <- t(Dineen_filtered)

  # Scale by row (z-score)
  expr_scaled <- t(scale(t(expr_matrix)))

  # Make sure comparison dataframe has all genes and is ordered correctly
  comparison <- comparison[match(rownames(expr_matrix), comparison$wbid), ]

  # Create row annotations for WGCNA modules
  module_colors <- comparison$wgcna_module
  names(module_colors) <- comparison$wbid

  # Create row annotations for DESeq2 patterns
  buffering_colors <- comparison$buffering_pattern

  # Define colors for buffering patterns
  buffering_palette <- c(
    "ELT7_buffers_upregulation" = "#E31A1C",
    "ELT7_buffers_downregulation" = "#1F78B4",
    "ELT7_compensatory" = "#33A02C",
    "ELT7_reveals_new_targets" = "#FF7F00",
    "ELT2_dominant_no_ELT7_effect" = "#999999",
    "other" = "#FFFFFF"
  )

  # Create row annotation for WGCNA modules (on left)
  row_ha_left <- rowAnnotation(
    WGCNA = module_colors,
    col = list(WGCNA = setNames(unique(module_colors), unique(module_colors))),
    annotation_name_side = "top"
  )

  # Create column annotations for genotypes
  col_ha <- HeatmapAnnotation(
    Genotype = c(rep("WT", 4), rep("elt7d", 3), rep("elt2d", 4), rep("double", 3)),
    col = list(Genotype = c("WT" = "#4DAF4A", "elt2d" = "#E41A1C",
                            "elt7d" = "#377EB8", "double" = "#984EA3"))
  )

  # Create the heatmap with row splitting by DESeq2 buffering pattern
  ht <- Heatmap(expr_scaled,
                name = "Expression\n(z-score)",

                # Clustering
                cluster_rows = TRUE,
                cluster_columns = FALSE,
                show_row_dend = TRUE,
                show_column_dend = FALSE,

                # # Split by DESeq2 buffering pattern
                # row_split = factor(buffering_colors,
                #                  levels = c("ELT7_buffers_upregulation",
                #                           "ELT7_buffers_downregulation",
                #                           "ELT7_compensatory",
                #                           "ELT7_reveals_new_targets",
                #                           "ELT2_dominant_no_ELT7_effect",
                #                           "other")),
                row_split = 9,
                row_title_rot = 0,
                row_gap = unit(2, "mm"),

                # Annotations
                left_annotation = row_ha_left,
                top_annotation = col_ha,

                # Labels
                show_row_names = FALSE,
                show_column_names = TRUE,
                column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),

                # Colors
                col = colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), c("blue", "white", "red")),

                # Size
                heatmap_legend_param = list(
                  title = "Expression\n(z-score)",
                  at = c(-2, 0, 2),
                  labels = c("-2", "0", "2")
                )
  )

  # Draw the heatmap
  draw(ht, heatmap_legend_side = "right")
```

```{r module descriptions}
 # Get module sizes
  module_sizes <- table(mergedColors)

  # Create summary table
  module_summary <- data.frame(
    Module = rownames(moduleTraitCor),
    n_genes = as.numeric(module_sizes[gsub("ME", "", rownames(moduleTraitCor))]),

    # Get strongest correlation
    strongest_cor = apply(abs(moduleTraitCor), 1, max),
    strongest_trait = colnames(moduleTraitCor)[apply(abs(moduleTraitCor), 1, which.max)],

    # Get the actual correlation value (with sign)
    cor_value = sapply(1:nrow(moduleTraitCor), function(i) {
      moduleTraitCor[i, which.max(abs(moduleTraitCor[i,]))]
    }),

    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  # Add short and long descriptions based on patterns
  module_summary$short_description <- c(
    "ELT-7Δ specific",           # MEblack: r=0.85 elt7d
    "WT/intestinal identity",    # MEblue: r=0.74 WT
    "Double mutant DOWN",        # MEpink: r=-0.97 double
    "Double mutant UP",          # MEgreen: r=0.99 double
    "ELT-2Δ DOWN, Double UP",   # MEtan: r=-0.72 elt2d, r=0.80 double
    "WT high, Double mid",       # MEgrey60: r=-0.85 WT
    "ELT-2Δ UP, Double mid",    # MEturquoise: r=0.64 elt2d
    "Unresponsive",              # MElightgreen: weak correlations
    "ELT-2Δ specific UP"         # MEred: r=0.97 elt2d
  )

  module_summary$long_description <- c(
    "Genes specifically upregulated in ELT-7Δ single mutant. May represent ELT-7 repressed targets or compensatory responses.",

    "Genes highly expressed in wild-type, maintained in normal intestinal identity. Reduced in ELT-2Δ mutants, suggesting ELT-2 activated targets.",

    "Genes dramatically downregulated specifically in double mutant (r=-0.97). Represents strong ELT-7 buffering - genes maintained in ELT-2Δ alone but lost when both 
  TFs deleted.",

    "Genes dramatically upregulated specifically in double mutant (r=0.99). May represent derepression when both ELT-2 and ELT-7 removed, or compensatory stress 
  responses.",

    "Genes downregulated in ELT-2Δ but upregulated in double mutant. Suggests complex regulatory logic where ELT-7 loss reverses ELT-2Δ effects.",

    "Genes high in WT and moderately high in double mutant. Represents partial compensation or alternative regulatory pathways.",

    "Genes upregulated in ELT-2Δ with moderate correlation in double. May represent indirect responses to ELT-2 loss.",

    "Genes with weak correlations to all genotypes. May be constitutively expressed or respond to technical variation rather than genotype.",

    "Genes strongly and specifically upregulated in ELT-2Δ (r=0.97), but not in double mutant. Classic ELT-2 repressed targets or compensatory upregulation that's 
  suppressed when ELT-7 also removed."
  )

  # Reorder by module size (largest first) or by trait association
  module_summary <- module_summary[order(-module_summary$n_genes), ]

  # Print the table
  print(module_summary, row.names = FALSE)

  # Save as CSV
  write.csv(module_summary, "WGCNA_module_annotations.csv", row.names = FALSE)

  # Create a nice formatted table view
  library(knitr)
  kable(module_summary[, c("Module", "n_genes", "strongest_trait", "cor_value", "short_description")],
        col.names = c("Module", "# Genes", "Primary Trait", "Correlation", "Description"),
        digits = 2)
```
